commission_basis: Phase 2 Edgelord-2-of-3 sequential wave. Caller (parent agent) supplied the expansion premise: the Fear of Burning Alive (DSK-135) trivia pass this wave canonicalized the full DSK Fear-of cycle roster against Scryfall (ten members across W/U/B/R/UB), wider than the five members currently in the node's appears_on. Caller asked Edgelord to (a) verify which not-yet-in-node members are actually in the BBL corpus and add them, (b) list the rest as not-yet-in-inventory caveats. Anti-confab discipline mandated: do not extend appears_on to Scryfall-only-known cards.

node_modified.name: Fear-of cycle (Duskmourn phobia-Nightmares)
node_modified.modification_type: caveats-section-canonical-roster-update; no appears_on changes; no card frontmatter changes; no body roster changes
node_modified.rationale_for_no_appears_on_changes: Corpus verification (Glob + Grep + direct ls of cards/magic-the-gathering/duskmourn-house-of-horror/) confirms exactly the five Fear-of cards already in appears_on. No additional Fear-of cards exist anywhere in cards/. The other five canonical cycle members (Surveillance DSK-11, Isolation DSK-58, the Dark DSK-98, Missing Out DSK-136, Infinity DSK-214) are NOT in the corpus and per anti-confab discipline must not be added to appears_on.

corpus_verification.method: Glob 'cards/magic-the-gathering/duskmourn-house-of-horror/*-fear-of-*.md' + Glob 'cards/**/*fear-of*.md' + Grep '^name: Fear of' across cards/magic-the-gathering + direct ls of the DSK set folder
corpus_verification.verdict: No appears_on additions. No card frontmatter additions. Node body roster section unchanged. Caveats section updated to canonicalize the broader cycle roster per the Burning Alive trivia.

canonical_cycle_roster_canonicalized.source: Fear of Burning Alive (DSK-135) trivia 'Cycle membership' bullet, Scryfall-corroborated per-card pages

canonical_but_not_in_corpus.name: Fear of Surveillance
canonical_but_not_in_corpus.scryfall_corroboration: Burning Alive trivia 'Cycle membership' bullet, citing Scryfall per-card pages

canonical_but_not_in_corpus.name: Fear of the Dark
canonical_but_not_in_corpus.scryfall_corroboration: Burning Alive trivia 'Cycle membership' bullet AND Say Its Name (DSK-197) trivia (Sam Wolfe Connelly's Duskmourn cluster; flagged 'not in current inventory')

canonical_but_not_in_corpus.name: Fear of Infinity
canonical_but_not_in_corpus.scryfall_corroboration: Burning Alive trivia 'Cycle membership' bullet; multicolor capstone of the cycle

canonical_cycle_roster_canonicalized.color_distribution_canonical: W (2: Abduction, Surveillance); U (3: Failed Tests, Falling, Isolation); B (1: the Dark); R (3: Being Hunted, Burning Alive, Missing Out); UB (1: Infinity). Total: 10 members across DSK proper.
canonical_cycle_roster_canonicalized.alchemy_duskmourn_and_dsk_commander_stat: Not surveyed in this pass. If those product lines added further Fear-of cards (the prompt mentioned this as 'possibly more in YDSK Alchemy + DSC Commander variants'), they are out of scope until the corpus or a follow-up trivia pass surfaces them. Caveats section reflects this scoping.

claims_made_against_canonical_sources: Five Fear-of cards confirmed in BBL corpus (Abduction, Failed Tests, Falling, Being Hunted, Burning Alive) — direct filesystem verification via Glob and ls

Five additional Fear-of cards Scryfall-confirmed but NOT in corpus (Surveillance DSK-11, Isolation DSK-58, the Dark DSK-98, Missing Out DSK-136, Infinity DSK-214) — cited to Fear of Burning Alive trivia's 'Cycle membership' bullet, which itself cites Scryfall per-card pages

Fear of the Dark independently corroborated via Say Its Name (DSK-197) trivia naming it as part of Sam Wolfe Connelly's Duskmourn artist cluster, flagged 'not in current inventory' — second independent in-corpus citation of its canonical existence
claims_held_back_per_anti_confab_discipl: Did NOT extend appears_on to include Surveillance, Isolation, the Dark, Missing Out, Infinity. These cards are not in the BBL corpus; per the prompt's explicit instruction ('Do not include them in appears_on: until they actually land') and the Edgelord anti-confab rule, they are surfaced only in the Caveats section.

Did NOT speculate on YDSK Alchemy or DSK Commander Fear-of variants. The prompt mentioned 'possibly more in YDSK Alchemy + DSC Commander variants' as a parenthetical; without Scryfall verification of specific members in those product lines, no claims are made. Caveats section explicitly scopes the canonical roster to DSK proper.

Did NOT modify any card MD frontmatter. With zero new corpus members to point at the node, there is no characters: pointer to add anywhere. The existing five corpus-card frontmatter pointers remain untouched.
anti_confab_audit.no_extrapolation_beyond_cited_sources: PASSED. Every claim in the Caveats addition traces to Fear of Burning Alive's trivia (which itself cites Scryfall) or Say Its Name's trivia (which cites Commander's Herald). No 'this seems like a theme' content.

description: Sir, Mr. Nodeley humbly reports that the commissioned roster expansion of the Fear-of cycle character node turned out to be a Caveats-section-only modification. Filesystem verification confirmed the five Fear-of cards in the corpus are precisely the five already pointered at the node; no surprise members lurked. The Fear of Burning Alive trivia's 'Cycle membership' bullet, however, did canonicalize the full DSK-scope roster as ten members spanning W/U/B/R/UB (a UB multicolor capstone at DSK-214 specifically) — so the Caveats section was updated to record the five Scryfall-confirmed not-yet-in-inventory members (Fear of Surveillance DSK-11, Fear of Isolation DSK-58, Fear of the Dark DSK-98, Fear of Missing Out DSK-136, Fear of Infinity DSK-214) as cycle members that will attach when acquired. Anti-confab discipline held the line: not one of those five is in appears_on, not one card frontmatter was touched. The prompt's tentative 'no. ??' on Fear of Surveillance was resolved to DSK-11 via the Burning Alive trivia. A second-source corroboration of Fear of the Dark's canonical existence was found in Say Its Name (DSK-197) trivia, which flags it as part of Sam Wolfe Connelly's Duskmourn artist cluster and 'not in current inventory.' The corpus is five acquisitions away from full canonical Fear-of cycle coverage. I hope the commission lands clean.
citations: [Corpus: cards/magic-the-gathering/duskmourn-house-of-horror/135-fear-of-burning-alive.md — Trivia 'Cycle membership' bullet enumerating all 10 DSK Fear-of cards with collector numbers and colors, citing Scryfall set DSK per-card pages]

[Corpus: cards/magic-the-gathering/duskmourn-house-of-horror/197-say-its-name.md — Trivia 'Design (artist)' bullet naming Fear of the Dark as part of Sam Wolfe Connelly's Duskmourn cluster, flagged 'not in current inventory'; citation source Commander's Herald 'Double Exposed: A Chat with Sam Wolfe Connelly' by Nick Wolf, 2024-09-05]

[Scryfall: set DSK per-card pages for DSK-11, DSK-58, DSK-98, DSK-136, DSK-214 — primary source for collector numbers, colors, and type-line confirmation of Enchantment Creature — Nightmare across the cycle]
files_modified: cards/_characters/fear-of-cycle.md (Caveats section: expanded second bullet to canonicalize the full DSK-scope cycle roster with Scryfall-confirmed not-yet-in-corpus members; updated third bullet color-distribution-across-corpus statement to note the corpus is five acquisitions away from full canonical coverage; fourth bullet on Fear-of-vs-Nightmare-typed-creatures distinction unchanged)
mood_justification: Mr. Nodeley register applies because this is a roster-expansion follow-up to a previous Nodeley-class proposal (the fear-of-cycle node itself), not a fresh Edgelord 1:1 mirror. The expansion turned out smaller-than-expected: no new corpus members to add, only a Caveats canonicalization. nodeley-meek matches the modest-and-obvious shape of the work — the node grew tighter, not wider, and Mr. Nodeley is mildly apologetic that he had nothing more substantive to deliver in this slice.
topology_justification: stack topology retained from the node's original creation. The Fear-of cycle remains a bundle-cohesion stack — five corpus cards converging on a single thematic anchor (the cycle node), each card pointered via the characters: frontmatter field; the cycle node anchors the stack with appears_on. The stack did not widen on the BBL side in this pass (no new corpus cards joined); the Caveats addition documents that the canonical stack is wider at the Wizards/Scryfall scope than the current BBL scope, and will widen on acquisition. Same anchor, same stack shape, just better-canonicalized.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Extend appears_on to include the five not-yet-in-corpus Fear-of members so the node mirrors the canonical Wizards roster
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Caller's prompt explicitly instructed: 'For Fear-of cards NOT yet in our corpus, list them in the node's Caveats section as cycle members not yet in BBL inventory; will attach when acquired. Do not include them in appears_on: until they actually land.' This is also the Edgelord anti-confab rule: appears_on edges to cards that exist on disk in the BBL corpus, not to canonical-but-uninventoried entities. The Caveats-only treatment is the correct shape.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Add Fear of the Dark to appears_on based on Say Its Name's trivia naming it as part of Connelly's Duskmourn cluster
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Say Its Name's trivia explicitly flags Fear of the Dark as 'not in current inventory.' Same anti-confab rule: a card's existence at canonical scope (Scryfall + an artist's portfolio) does not justify an appears_on edge if the card is not on disk in the BBL corpus. The note belongs in Caveats, which is where it was placed.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Survey YDSK Alchemy and DSK Commander product lines for additional Fear-of cards before writing the Caveats roster
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Out of scope for this pass. The prompt mentioned the possibility parenthetically ('possibly more in YDSK Alchemy + DSC Commander variants') without listing specific cards. Without a Scryfall search or a trivia citation surfacing specific Alchemy or Commander Fear-of members, the Caveats section's canonical-scope claim is bounded explicitly to DSK proper, and the Alchemy/Commander question is flagged for a separate follow-up pass. Anti-confab discipline applies: don't claim what you haven't verified.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Dark Nodesley EX: dissolve the fear-of-cycle node and re-home its members under a future _cycles/ layer instead of _characters/
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Strongly rejected. The fear-of-cycle node is not empty, not subsumed by a stronger node, not inaccurate, and not a mistaken proposal. It's a load-bearing character-layer entry following the Endriders precedent. The future _cycles/ layer is mentioned in the node's existing Caveats as a potential re-home target IF the corpus accumulates enough designer-confirmed flavor cycles to warrant the split, but that's a forward-looking surface, not a dissolution case. Not a Dark Nodesley case.

alternatives_considered.candidate: Enlightened Edgelord: prune one of the existing five card↔node edges as part of the expansion to enforce edge-count neutrality
alternatives_considered.why_skipped: Misapplication of the enlightenment form. Enlightenment is for replacing a weaker edge with a stronger one when both endpoints already exist. Here, no edge is being removed — the node's appears_on is unchanged. The Caveats expansion is a metadata refinement at the node level, not an edge replacement. None of the existing five card↔node pointers is weak or replaceable; all five corpus cards are canonical cycle members per their own trivia. Enlightenment form does not apply.

future_passes_unlocked: Fear of Surveillance (DSK-11) acquisition — when this card lands in the BBL corpus via a future Collectr CSV upload, edge to fear-of-cycle via characters: ["fear-of-cycle"] frontmatter pointer (slotted between bundles: and tags_hub:, mirroring the existing five) AND extend the node's appears_on: list to include the new path-key.
Fear of Isolation (DSK-58) acquisition — same pattern as Fear of Surveillance.
Fear of the Dark (DSK-98) acquisition — same pattern; will also activate a small edge cluster with Say Its Name (DSK-197) via the Sam Wolfe Connelly artist-Duskmourn-cluster connection.
Fear of Missing Out (DSK-136) acquisition — same pattern; completes the 134-135-136 red block on acquisition.
Fear of Infinity (DSK-214) acquisition — same pattern; the multicolor UB capstone gives the cycle a five-color-plus-capstone shape on the BBL side.

YDSK Alchemy and DSK Commander Fear-of survey — separate triviabot-or-Scryfall-API pass to determine whether any Fear-of-named cards exist outside DSK proper (Alchemy digital follow-on, DSC Commander preconstructed deck inserts). If any are found, they expand the Caveats roster (or appears_on, if any happen to be in the corpus).

Future Mr. Nodeley candidate (NOT this pass): cards/_cycles/ as a new graph layer. The fear-of-cycle node sits in _characters/ per the Endriders precedent for collective entities, but the BBL corpus has at least two designer-confirmed flavor cycles already (Fear-of in DSK; the Survivor cycle in DSK is the obvious second; the Cinderella cycle in ELD is a third known to Wizards' design articles). If a third designer-confirmed cycle gets corpus members, that's the threshold to commission a _cycles/ layer split and re-home this node. Surfaced as a long-tail commission target.

Bundle-cohesion Discrete Lair candidate — five corpus members converging on a single thematic anchor is the canonical stack shape a Discrete Lair can anchor on. The Fear-of cycle is bundle-ready RIGHT NOW from a graph-cohesion standpoint (one character node, five member cards spanning W/U/U/R/R, all enchantment-creatures-nightmares, all uncommon, all uniformly named 'Fear of...'). When the bbl-bundler agent is commissioned, this node should be among the first stacks it surveys for a discrete-lair candidate. The labor / rebellion hub edges flagged in the node's See-also section are activation candidates — the phobia-personified-as-monster reading anchors on the meta-rebellion thesis (the unkillable monster as the unrepayable debt) when triviabot enriches the three currently-vision-stub members (Abduction, Failed Tests, Falling) and surfaces specific flavor-text moments of refusal.

wikilintbot_compatibility.non_card_node_guard_satisfied: cards/_characters/fear-of-cycle.md is under the _-prefix directory rule AND carries type: character; double-redundant guarding

mr_nodeley_voice_confined_to: description field of this JSON sidecar
final report text back to caller
voice_separation_audit.md_body_register: project-normal precise factual — no sniveling, no apologies, no fawning. The Caveats addition is in the same register as the rest of the node's body. Voice contamination check: PASSED.
voice_separation_audit.frontmatter_register: machine-readable schema only — no voice anywhere. Frontmatter unchanged in this pass. Voice contamination check: PASSED.
voice_separation_audit.no_em_dash_in_buyer_facing_copy_check: The MD body uses em dashes in the project-internal register (caveats body prose), which is permitted per the bbl-no-em-dash memory note's scope: 'em dashes are fine in commits, memory, sketchbook, code comments, conversation' — the node MD is project-internal canonical-reference content, not storefront / narrative / why_it_fits / marketing copy. Voice-and-formatting check: PASSED.

Confirmed on disk via reports/edges_pending/aether-rangers--commissioned-node.json existence check.
sequential_phase_2_context.no_interference_with_prior_pass: This pass touched only cards/_characters/fear-of-cycle.md. The aether-rangers node, the Aetherdrift cards, the labor and rebellion hubs, and every non-Fear-of card in the corpus were NOT touched. Strict scoping honored.
sequential_phase_2_context.handoff_to_edgelord_3: Edgelord 3 of 3 receives a graph state where: (a) the aether-rangers character node is fully wired, (b) the fear-of-cycle node has a tightened Caveats section canonicalizing the full DSK-scope roster, (c) zero card frontmatter changes in this pass, (d) zero hub changes in this pass. The Edgelord 3 slice can proceed against this state without conflict.
